## Description / Excerpt

A category paper defining Constitutional AI Infrastructure and a governance maturity model for moving regulated organizations from raw AI outputs to auditable, time-stable, invariant-constrained decision systems.

Excerpt:

--- ## Executive Summary Artificial intelligence has been rapidly adopted across regulated industries. Yet most deployments focus on output generation rather than structural governance. As a result, organizations face a widening gap between accelerated decision-making and the abi

## Working Read

This is the cleanest governance maturity model in the corpus because it moves from raw outputs to auditable, time-stable decision systems. It sharpens the governance attractor around legitimacy, drift management, and regulated accountability.

## Core Claim

Governance must make regulated decision-making legible, invariant-constrained, and accountable over time instead of leaving AI as an output machine.
